qas.q: If you are already carrying 100 pounds of silver cyanide, what precautions must you take if you are given papers at dock to carry 100 cartons of battery acid?
qas.a: Inform someone and not load the battery acid

qas.q: A placarded vehicle must carry what type of fire extinguisher?
qas.a: One with a rating of 10 B:C minimum

qas.q: What action should you take if there is no phone available and you discover your hazardous materials shipment leaking at a rest stop?
qas.a: Send someone for help with all the necessary information

qas.q: Which of the following is NOT something you need to know in order to determine if you need to use placards?
qas.a: The manufacturing date for the materials

qas.q: If you are carrying Division 1.2 or 1.3 materials, how far away must you park from the traveled portion of the roadway?
qas.a: At least 5 feet

qas.q: In what location must you keep your shipping papers which describe any hazardous materials?
qas.a: On the driver's seat anytime you are outside of the vehicle

qas.q: Which of the following materials would be acceptable floor liner for moving Division 1.1 or 1.2 materials?
qas.a: Non-ferrous metal

qas.q: Your engine runs a pump when you are delivering compressed gas. Should you turn off your engine before or after you unhook the hoses after finishing that delivery?
qas.a: Turn it off before unhooking

qas.q: Which of the following hazard classes utilizes a transport index in order to determine how much of it can be loaded on a single vehicle for transport?
qas.a: Class 7 - Radioactive materials

qas.q: Cargo tanks are...
qas.a: Bulk packaging permanently attached to your vehicle

qas.q: Which hazard classes must you NEVER smoke, or perform any activity involving fire, within 25 feet of?
qas.a: Classes 1, 3, 4 and 4.2

qas.q: What is a technical name?
qas.a: The name for a hazardous material used in scientific journals and texts, recognized as its chemical and microbiological name

qas.q: What are shippers trying to accomplish when they package the material?
qas.a: Make it easy to identify

qas.q: A safe haven is...
qas.a: A place that has been approved to park unattended vehicles carrying explosives

qas.q: How far away must you stay from a bridge, tunnel, or building if you are carrying Division 1.2 or 1.3 materials?
qas.a: 300 feet or more

qas.q: Which of the following is NOT an acceptable type of marking for hazardous materials?
qas.a: Name in italics

qas.q: Do you need to stop before a railroad crossing if you are hauling 100 pounds of Division 4.3 materials?
qas.a: Yes

qas.q: The two other places where the hazardous identification number must appear are...
qas.a: Any bulk packaging and the cargo tanks

qas.q: What is the purpose of a driver placarding his or her vehicle?
qas.a: Communicating risk

qas.q: How often should you check the tires on a placarded trailer that has dual tires?
qas.a: Start of each day and every time you stop

qas.q: Where are the two main places where the hazardous identification number appear?
qas.a: On the shipping paper and on the package

qas.q: Which of the following three hazard classes should NOT be placed into a temperature-controlled trailer (one with a heater/air conditioner unit)?
qas.a: Classes 1, 2.1 and 3
